title: ancistrophyllum secundiflorum
intro: Ancistrophyllum secundiflorum, also known as the secondary-flowered club moss, is a species of plant in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in West Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. Ancistrophyllum secundiflorum is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
